title: DON'T COUGH ON ME
description:
The snake represents the mortal coil of each patriot. The "I" of the snake is actually a "we", as in "we the people". 

To transcend our physical coil, we dress ourselves in symbols that keep the highest aim in mind and at hand.  

The American flag flies high and true above the snake's head, faithfully reflecting an inner patriotism. These colors don't run, and these lines don't blur. 

Stemming from the same flagpole that flies the outer flag, is a brain. The brain arises from genetic causes, which themselves come from the inner flag.

The two main fangs of the snake are the 1st and 2nd amendment -- Either we have both Speech & Arms, or we have neither. 

The two branches of the tongue of the snake represent the conjoined status of Tree of Freedom & Free Speech -- these twins live free and die hard, together. 

Also, unlike most snakes which threaten with the tail and bite with the mouth, this snake can also sting (a trick it learned from the bees). 

Don't cough on me or my rights, this snake stings and this snake bites!
